name: Tayba Foundation
mission:
Tayba aims to become a pioneer—the first to offer a complete, high-quality online curriculum featuring engaging visual and audio instruction Our programs SOURCE: Self-reported by organization What are the organization's current programs, how do they measure success,
            and who do the programs serve? Education At Tayba, we believe that character reformation gives incarcerated clients and the formerly incarcerated the tools they need to successfully reintegrate into society following parole.
theoryOfChange: Tayba Foundation states that through thoughtful, well-designed programs in Education, Life Skills, and Reentry, clients acquire tools needed to unlock human potential and forge a healthy future; mentoring relationships and continuing education are emphasized for successful reentry.
scoreSummary: TAYBA FOUNDATION shows exceptional impact and strong alignment, with zakat compliance.

all_citations.claim: The organization has a Charity Navigator score of 94.25/100, a program expense ratio of 84.6%, 6.7 months of working capital, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.18 per $1 raised.
The Tayba Foundation is Zakat-eligible and maintains specific policies regarding Zakat distribution.
The organization reported total revenue of $1,460,391 in its most recent financial filings.
Tayba offers an online curriculum and re-entry programs focused on character reformation and reducing recidivism.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a high overall score due to its exceptional financial health, transparency, and specialized focus on domestic poverty and prisoner rehabilitation.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing direct impact measurement to further validate the cost per beneficiary of approximately $5,469 [1].
Expanding donor alignment strategies to move beyond a medium fit for the target audience [1].

dimension_explanations.alignment: Tayba Foundation shows strong alignment with domestic poverty relief and is officially Zakat-eligible, adhering to specific distribution policies for asnaf categories [2]. It offers a medium donor fit for those seeking highly specialized domestic interventions and maintains high data confidence through verified financial filings [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high impact through its capacity-building model, maintaining a Charity Navigator score of 94.25/100 and a program expense ratio of 84.6% [1]. While the cost per beneficiary is calculated at approximately $5,469, the organization's focus on non-recidivism provides a high-value societal return [4].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Tayba Foundation provides high-quality educational curricula and holistic re-entry support to empower incarcerated and formerly incarcerated individuals.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 84.6%, indicating a strong commitment to direct service delivery [1].
Maintains a healthy financial position with 6.7 months of working capital [1].
Strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.18 to raise every $1 [1].
baseline_narrative.summary: The Tayba Foundation is a non-profit organization dedicated to character reformation through a comprehensive online curriculum featuring visual and audio instruction [4]. With a focus on education, life skills, and re-entry services, the foundation aims to provide clients with the necessary tools to successfully reintegrate into society following parole [4].

bbb_assessment.summary: Maintains high internal governance standards and independent board oversight.

case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="1">high cost per beneficiary</cite> and the niche focus which limits the total number of individuals served compared to larger humanitarian agencies.

confidence.notes: Strong alignment between financial filings and third-party ratings.

alignment.explanation: Strong fit for Muslim donors with <cite id="2">verified zakat eligibility</cite> serving the fuqara, masakin, and riqab (those in bondage/incarcerated) categories.
alignment.improvement: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors to improve alignment scores.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="1">94.94.25/100 from Charity Navigator rating</cite> and its <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
credibility.improvement: Credibility could be further enhanced by publishing more frequent independent impact evaluations.

impact.explanation: Operations show a <cite id="1">84.6% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="4">100% non-recidivism rate</cite> for participants.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">tracking cost-per-beneficiary</cite> more granularly across its four main program areas.

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Direct service delivery rather than grantmaking

rich_narrative.headline: The Tayba Foundation provides high-quality educational curricula and holistic re-entry support to empower incarcerated and formerly incarcerated individuals.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support the spiritual and social rehabilitation of incarcerated Muslims in the US through high-quality education and re-entry support.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations toward the 'riqab' (those in bondage) category
Believe in the power of Islamic education for character reformation
Value domestic US-based programs with measurable social outcomes
Prefer supporting niche, underserved populations over large-scale relief
giving_considerations: Gifts support intensive, high-cost individual interventions rather than mass aid
The organization is an established institution with 15+ years of experience
Focus is strictly on the US prison system and domestic re-entry
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking international emergency relief or those who prioritize the lowest possible cost-per-beneficiary metrics.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified outcome metrics like non-recidivism rates and a documented theory of change.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Tayba uses Islamic education to reform character, which combined with life skills and re-entry support, prevents recidivism.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence of zero recidivism proves that educational intervention is a viable alternative to traditional punitive measures.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 3-year revenue CAGR of 11.2%, the organization is growing but serves only a fraction of the incarcerated Muslim population.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="4">specialized online curriculum</cite> and focus on the 'riqab' zakat category.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses more on prison visitation and mentorship
similar_organizations.name: Link Outside

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on pre-trial bail and ending cash bail
similar_organizations.name: Believers Bail Out

strengths.detail: The organization holds a <cite id="1">94.25/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="1">perfect 100 score for Culture and Leadership</cite>.
Tayba directs <cite id="1">84.6% of its expenses toward direct program services</cite>, ensuring donor funds reach the intended beneficiaries.
The foundation reports a <cite id="4">100% non-recidivism rate</cite> for its clients, demonstrating the effectiveness of its holistic support model.

strengths_deep_dive: Tayba Foundation's operational strength is rooted in its financial resilience and transparency. The organization maintains <cite id="1">6.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term program stability. Their governance is robust, featuring a <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> and a <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>. Furthermore, their fundraising strategy is remarkably efficient, utilizing only <cite id="1">$0.06 to $0.18 per $1 raised</cite> depending on the reporting metric used, which is well below industry averages for organizations of this size.
rich_narrative.summary: The Tayba Foundation operates as a specialized educational and re-entry institution serving incarcerated and formerly incarcerated individuals across the United States. The organization provides a <cite id="4">comprehensive online curriculum featuring visual and audio instruction</cite> designed for character reformation and spiritual growth. Unlike many general humanitarian charities, Tayba focuses on the niche intersection of Islamic education and criminal justice reform. They maintain a <cite id="1">94.25/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting high standards of accountability and financial health. In 2023, the foundation reported <cite id="3">$1,460,391 in total revenue</cite>, primarily driven by public contributions. Their operational model is lean, with a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 84.6%</cite> and a highly efficient fundraising operation that spends only <cite id="1">$0.18 to raise every $1</cite>. Beyond education, the foundation provides <cite id="4">life skills and re-entry support</cite> to ensure successful reintegration into society. This holistic approach has yielded significant results, including a reported <cite id="4">100% non-recidivism rate</cite> among their tracked beneficiary population. While the cost per beneficiary is approximately $5,469, this reflects the intensive, long-term nature of their educational and support services compared to one-time relief efforts.
